title: PM Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana: Free Food Grains for 81 Crore People
meta_description: Get free rations under PM Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana: 5kg rice/wheat + 1kg dal monthly. Eligibility, portability & how to apply.

hero.one_liner: Free monthly rations for 81 crore poor families across India till December 2028

what_is_it: The PM Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY) is the world's largest free food program. The government gives every poor family in India free rice or wheat and lentils every month. As long as you hold a ration card and your name is on it, you can walk to your nearest fair price shop and collect free grains worth ₹200-300 per month. This continues until December 2028. No payment, no conditions, just food.

The scheme covers 81.35 crore people—that's more than half of India's population. It includes both Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) households (the poorest) who get 35 kg per month, and Priority Households (PHH) who get 5 kg per person per month, plus 1 kg dal for the whole family. In practical terms, a family of four gets 20 kg rice/wheat and 1 kg dal monthly for zero cost.

This scheme started during COVID-19 in 2020 as an emergency measure, but it was so successful that the government extended it indefinitely. The government has allocated ₹2,03,000 crore in the 2025-26 budget just for this scheme. It's designed to eliminate hunger and give every poor family guaranteed access to basic food.

One of the smartest features is portability under ONORC (One Nation One Ration Card). You don't have to collect rations only from your home state. If you migrate to another state for work, you can carry your ration card and collect the same free grains from fair price shops anywhere in India. This breaks the old system where migrant workers lost their ration card benefits.

eligibility.label: Ration Card
eligibility.value: Must possess a valid ration card (NFSA beneficiary)

eligibility.label: Age Requirement
eligibility.value: Any age (families with children, elderly, adults all covered)

eligibility.label: Family Income
eligibility.value: Below poverty line or low-income eligible under NFSA criteria

eligibility.label: Indian Citizen
eligibility.value: Must be Indian citizen with valid state residency

eligibility.label: No Income Limit Upper Bound
eligibility.value: If on ration card, you're eligible regardless of other income sources

extra_sections.heading: What You Get Every Month
rows: Not per person—35 kg per household

extra_sections.heading: ONORC: Get Rations Anywhere in India

steps.title: Understand What ONORC Means
steps.description: One Nation One Ration Card means your ration card works in any state. If your family is from Bihar but you work in Mumbai, you can use your Bihar ration card to get free rations in Mumbai. This is a game-changer for migrant workers and families separated by work.

steps.title: Check if Your State is ONORC-Enabled
steps.description: Most states now have ONORC implemented. Check on the NFSA website or your state's food and public distribution department website to confirm. If your state isn't listed yet, it should be soon as this is a nationwide rollout.

steps.title: Get Your Ration Card Linked to Aadhaar
steps.description: ONORC portability works best when your ration card and Aadhaar are linked. Visit your local ration shop or food office and request Aadhaar-ration card seeding. This is free and takes a few minutes. Your cardholders' names and Aadhaar numbers must match.

steps.title: Locate a Fair Price Shop in Your New State
steps.description: Go to nfsa.gov.in and search for fair price shops in your new location (enter district and block). You'll see a list with shop names and addresses. Note down the closest one.

steps.title: Visit the Fair Price Shop with Your Ration Card
steps.description: Bring your ration card and Aadhaar. Show them to the shop owner. They'll verify your details in their system. If everything matches, you'll get your monthly free rations on the spot.

steps.title: Check Your Record if There's a Problem
steps.description: If the shopkeeper says your card isn't in the system, ask them to check using your Aadhaar number. Sometimes records take time to sync across states. If it's been days and still not showing, contact the food and public distribution office of your new state.

extra_sections.heading: Common Problems at Fair Price Shops and Solutions
content:
Shop Says 'Card Not in System' or 'Not Our District'

This is common because records take time to sync across states. First, ask the shop to check again using your Aadhaar number instead of card number.
If still not found, contact the district food office and ask them to sync your card to the new state's system. This is their responsibility and is usually free.
Don't give up—it's a technical issue, not a permanent problem.

Getting Less Grain Than Entitled

Some corrupt shopkeepers give less grain and pocket the difference. You're entitled to the full amount.
Count the grain in front of the shopkeeper and weigh it if possible. If short, tell the shopkeeper immediately.
If they refuse to add more, go to the district food office and file a complaint with the date and amount short. This will be investigated.

Shop Refuses to Give Free Rations and Asks for Money

PMGKAY rations are completely free. If a shopkeeper demands money or says you must buy at regular price, report this immediately to the district food office.
Get the shop's name and owner's name. This is illegal and the government takes it seriously.

Your Ration Card Has Wrong Name or Number of Family Members

If the card shows 3 family members but you have 4, you get less ration. Visit the ration office (food and supplies office) with all family members' Aadhaar cards and request a correction.
Don't delay—every month you lose your share.

Grain Quality is Bad (Stones, Worms, or Moldy)

Poor quality grain is grounds for complaint. Don't accept it.
Tell the shopkeeper you want fresh stock and report it to the district food office. If the shopkeeper refuses, write a letter to the Block Development Officer (BDO) and the District Magistrate.
Include the date, grain type, and details. Government has to investigate quality complaints.

Shop's Opening Hours Don't Work for Your Job

Fair price shops usually open fixed hours. If you can't collect during those times, ask if the shopkeeper can give you an alternate date.
If refused, contact the food office. Some shops have flexibility, especially for working people.
Alternatively, check if a different shop near your workplace is available.

extra_sections.heading: PMGKAY vs Old NFSA Pricing
rows: ₹3 per kg for AAY, ₹2 per kg for APL
Available at subsidized rates; not always free
Reduced but families still struggled during crises
Virtually eliminates hunger; enough for basic meals
Partially available; some states didn't cooperate

extra_sections.heading: Government's Commitment and Budget
content: The government has allocated ₹2,03,000 crore for the PM Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana in the 2025-26 budget. This is a massive commitment—more than many state budgets.
The scheme is guaranteed to continue till December 2028, and there are strong indications it will continue beyond that.

To put this in perspective: ₹2,03,000 crore divided among 81.35 crore beneficiaries works out to roughly ₹2,500 per person per year, or about ₹200+ per person per month. That's real, tangible food support for over half of India's population.
No other food welfare program in the world reaches this scale.
The scheme is funded from central taxes and budgetary allocations. Every Indian taxpayer effectively contributes to feeding poor families.
The government views this as an investment in nutrition, health, and social stability.

how_to_apply.title: Check if You're Already a NFSA Beneficiary
how_to_apply.description: You don't need to apply for PMGKAY separately. If you already have a ration card (NFSA beneficiary), you're automatically eligible. Just walk to your nearest fair price shop with your ration card. However, if you don't have a ration card, you must first get one.

how_to_apply.title: Apply for a Ration Card if You Don't Have One
how_to_apply.description: Visit your district's food and public distribution office (called Ration Office, Food & Supplies Department, or Civil Supplies office—names vary by state). Fill out the NFSA beneficiary application form. You'll need: Aadhaar, proof of residence (electricity bill or lease agreement), and income proof (not required in some states).

how_to_apply.title: Get Your Documents Verified
how_to_apply.description: The food office will verify your information and possibly visit your home to check. This is to ensure the ration card goes to someone genuinely poor. Verification usually takes 15-30 days.

how_to_apply.title: Receive Your Ration Card
how_to_apply.description: Once approved, you'll get a ration card. This card has your family members' names, your card type (AAY or PHH), and your card number. The card is valid for several years.

how_to_apply.title: Link Your Ration Card with Aadhaar (Strongly Recommended)
how_to_apply.description: Go to your local ration shop with the card and all family members' Aadhaar cards. Ask the shopkeeper to seed (link) your Aadhaar to your ration card. This makes portability easier and prevents fraud. It's free and takes 5-10 minutes.

how_to_apply.title: Find Your Assigned Fair Price Shop
how_to_apply.description: Your ration card will mention which fair price shop serves your area. Locate it—usually within 1-2 km of your home. Or search on nfsa.gov.in by entering your state, district, and block.

how_to_apply.title: Collect Your First Month's Rations
how_to_apply.description: Visit the fair price shop with your ration card. Tell the shopkeeper it's your first time. Show your card. They'll verify your details in the system and give you your monthly rations. From then on, just show up on collection days.

how_to_apply.title: Plan to Collect Every Month
how_to_apply.description: Fair price shops usually collect rations on fixed days (e.g., 1st to 10th of every month). Don't delay—grain sometimes runs out if you come too late. Mark the collection dates on your calendar so you don't miss them.

important_alert.text: This Scheme is Completely Free: You do not pay anything for PMGKAY rations. Not even a rupee. If anyone—shopkeeper, official, or broker—asks for money, it is illegal. Report them immediately to the district food office or the civil supplies department. Keep the shopkeeper's name and shop details. Free rations are your right till December 2028.

important_dates.label: Collection Frequency
important_dates.value: Monthly (dates vary by state and shop)

important_dates.label: ONORC Rollout Status
important_dates.value: Live in most states, expanding nationwide

faqs.question: Will this scheme really continue till 2028 or beyond?
faqs.answer: The government has officially committed to December 2028. The Union Budget 2025-26 shows ₹2,03,000 crore allocation just for this scheme, which signals serious commitment. After 2028, the government will likely review and extend it again. It's too popular to discontinue, especially since it eliminates the single biggest cause of poverty—hunger.

faqs.question: What if my family has a job and earns ₹30,000-50,000 per month?
faqs.answer: If you're already on an NFSA ration card, income from employment doesn't disqualify you. The scheme doesn't perform continuous income checks. However, if you become wealthy enough to owe income tax, you should voluntarily withdraw from the scheme and let someone genuinely poor use that slot. But there's no government mechanism to force this.

faqs.question: If I migrate to another state for work, do I lose my rations?
faqs.answer: Not with ONORC. Carry your ration card to your new state, find a fair price shop in your new location, and you can collect the same free rations. Your card works nationwide. This is one of the biggest benefits for migrant workers. Make sure your ration card and Aadhaar are linked for smooth portability.

faqs.question: My shop always gives me poor quality grain. What can I do?
faqs.answer: This is illegal. Don't accept bad grain. Complain immediately to the district food office in writing. Include the shop's name, date, grain type, and details of the problem (stones, worms, mold, etc.). The government investigates such complaints and can replace the shopkeeper or supply better grain. You deserve good quality, not leftovers.

faqs.question: If someone in my family becomes a government employee, do I lose PMGKAY benefits?
faqs.answer: Not immediately. However, if you're a high-earning government employee, you should ethically withdraw and let someone poorer take your place. The government doesn't have active checks, but if discovered during an audit, there could be complications. It's best to inform the food office if your circumstances improve significantly.

faqs.question: How is the ration calculated if my family size changes?
faqs.answer: You need to update your ration card when family size changes (newborn, marriage, or death). Visit the food office and inform them. For PHH, you get 5 kg per person, so more family members = more rations. For AAY, you get fixed 35 kg per household. Update cards to avoid losing out on entitlements.

faqs.question: Why doesn't PMGKAY include protein foods like eggs, milk, or fish?
faqs.answer: PMGKAY focuses on staple grains that provide calories and basic nutrition. Adding perishables like eggs or milk would be logistically complex for fair price shops. Instead, the government runs separate nutrition programs for children (mid-day meals) and pregnant women (supplementary nutrition). The combination covers basic nutritional needs.

faqs.question: Can I sell my free rations instead of eating them?
faqs.answer: Legally, no. Rations are meant for personal consumption. However, many poor families do sell some rations to meet other urgent needs (medicine, schooling costs). The government doesn't police this heavily, but it's technically against the scheme rules. The intent is that you eat the grain, not that you go hungry while selling them.
